nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "You dare return human?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: You finally lay eyes on the creature. She is no beast, but a towering, ancient creature. She looks at you. "I sensed your presence since the moment you arrived. I was beginning to wonder when you humans would dare to pay me a visit."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Listen closely, for I do not repeat myself. Turn back to your ships. Return to your fleeting lives. If you remain here... you will die."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "We don't have to fight, we can talk first."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] P-please! Have mercy, oh Great Goddess! "

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] That's it? That's your threat? I expected more."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] You're older than I expected."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 7] You're ancient. Centuries old, at least. You existed long before the Shattering of the Verge."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Absolute Ineptitude] Wow... I didn't expect you to be hot. This complicates matters."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John stares at you, mouth slightly open. " Kid, generally I ignore your... special moments. But... fuck. Now it's not the time."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa covers her face with one hand. " Oh Herald... you do disappoint me occasionally."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] You sensed my presence... do you mean I smell bad? Oh Zeus, I really need a bath."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: the creature's eyes turn red. "Silence, insect. I am Malakor, the Living Foundation, do not speak to me as you would a common human."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Your words are wind. I have no interest in talking with inferior beings such as yourself."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: The creature gaze shifts to the old soldier. "But I do smell something familiar... John was it? I remember your brother screams. You are the one who ran, twenty years ago. The coward who left his kin to rot."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Yes that was me. I did not-... I'm glad you remember me. I'm known for giving an impression."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Malakor turns her massive head toward the Cleric. "you... Heretic. You wear that sigil on your chest and speak of mercy? Do not lie to me."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa looks at you, scared, then at Malakor "Filthy creature! It is not enough that you defile this land... you dare to lie! This is the star of Eirene! It represents peace and—" Malakor cuts her off.

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "I have no interest in what you have to say Heretic. "

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Malakor looks at you " you, Jade, that's your name? I have seen you in my dreams. That's the reason why, I give you the chance of running away."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: The monster grows larger and more terrifying. It is on a completely different level from anything you have encountered before. You feel in your bones that you currently have no chance of winning.

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John grabs your shoulder "Kid! Look at it! That ain't a fight that we can win! We can't win this. We need to run, now! I will explain later, but we can't win this."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa takes a step back "I... we have to run right now. Trust on me on this. Herald, we must retreat! The prophecy does not demand our deaths today!"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I don't care how big she is. I'm not running away. We end this now!"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " We're outmatched. [Run away is recommended for new players]"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: " We defeated Malakor {Debug}"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "How could a mere human defeat me"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John leans heavily on his weapon, panting. "Turns out even ancient horrors bleed when you hit them hard enough. Who knew?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa wipes a big smile on her face "Oh, look at the big monster now! This horrible filth is finally dying."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "You talked a big game for someone who just got beaten by a 'mere human'."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Wait, did we win? I closed my eyes halfway through."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] Your crystals were destroyed. We severed your connection to the neighboring islands. It's over"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] You were weak. As simple as that."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Malakor coughs "Fools... you celebrate your victory, oblivious to the doom you have brought upon yourselves. You have just destroyed your own world."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "And it is all because of your blind arrogance, child... and this weak, pathetic man cowering at your side."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "And it is all because of your blind arrogance, child."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John frowns, his grip tightening on his weapon. "Pardon? Mind explaining that before you choke on your own blood, you ugly bastard?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Your brother... He understood the truth. He let himself be killed to keep me bound, to keep this island whole."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "But you... you are far dumber than your older brother. You have undone his sacrifice out of sheer, pathetic ignorance."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John's face drops "Maxwell... he knew? The crystals... No... he told me to run... that can't be..."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa erupts into a piercing, unhinged cackle. "Oh, do you hear that, Herald? The foundations are unmade! All of it, crumbling into the void! Countless lives, all that crawling filth... finally being wiped clean! It's perfectly beautiful!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Malakor glares at the cleric. "You are the greatest fool of all, human. You wear the symbol of the Peacebringer, yet you celebrate the death of millions. You are a puppet. You reek of Ares, the God of War."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa's laughter stops, "Oh, Malakor, don't insult me. I know exactly who I serve." She rips the Eirene pendant from her neck and tosses it away. "I told you, didn't I? A garden cannot grow if it is choked by weeds. And this entire world... is nothing but filth. Lord Ares demands it all be purged in glorious chaos!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: The creature shakes its head. "You trade one delusion for another. There is no victory in this destruction. Even you will die by the Shattering of the world."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She smiles brightly. "Oh, I don't care about surviving, you overgrown lizard! I just wanted to watch it all drown in blood! And you... you were just the biggest weed of them all, standing in the way of true, absolute peace."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Wait... so all that talk about the Herald... the prophecy? It was all a lie?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] You manipulated us. You couldn't do it yourself, so you needed someone strong enough to break the crystals and kill the Living Foundation for you."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Wait... does this mean you're not going to heal me anymore?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa claps her hands together gleefully. "Of course it was a lie, my dear! The 'Herald of Eirene'? How could someone believe in that... I just needed a blunt instrument stupid enough to do the heavy lifting. And Jade, you were the perfect, gullible weapon to finally bring an end to this wretched world!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: The creature lets out a deep groan "You have been played by a madwoman. You have delivered this entire realm into oblivion."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Enough riddles, Malakor. Explain what the hell is actually going to happen now!"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] If you and the crystals were the tethers... what happens to the islands now that they are gone?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] Speak clearly before I crush your skull. What is happening to the world?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "A century ago, your kind arrived upon the Verge. You built your homes... and claimed the land as your own. Yet, my kin and I showed you mercy."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "We allowed you to stay. We even permitted you to harvest the surface minerals to build your little empires. If only you did as promised and stayed in your lane."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "But human greed is a bottomless pit. You lied. You marched your armies across the border to steal the very heart of this island. We did not start the Verge War. We were simply defending our home from parasites."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "For defending ourselves, you called us monsters... "

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I didn't know... We were the invaders all along. I'm sorry."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 7] I don't care about your history lesson! Humanity survives because we take what we need!"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Wait, so all this fighting... and there's no treasure at the end?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] It was a resource war. We mined the structural integrity of the island, and you fought back to prevent ecological collapse."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] I just wanted to survive... I didn't want to destroy the world! Please, can we fix it?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: The ground begins to shake violently. "Your words mean nothing now. The damage is done. I'm dying."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John drops his weapon "I was there... Maxwell and I stood side-by-side in the dirt. We thought we were righteous. We thought we were fighting for the gods, defending our homes... but we were just greedy thieves."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Clarissa throws her head back, laughing wildly over the rumbling earth. "Oh, the irony! The great, honorable heroes were just glittering locusts! Your precious war was a joke, Uncle! But it doesn't matter who started it, only how beautifully it ends!"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John stares at her, his voice cracking with heavy grief. "He was your father, Clarissa. He gave his life thinking he was saving you. Why on earth did you lie about this..."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "He was a weak, blind fool! Just like you!" Clarissa looks at you. "And you couldn't waste an opportunity to be the big hero, could you? You aren't greedy for gold, just greedy for the chance to feel bigger than the small, pathetic being you are."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: John lowers his head in defeat. "You're completely gone. My little niece died a long time ago... and I just helped a madwoman end the world."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: She hesitates for a moment. "Uncle... It's the only way to true peace. Ares promised me. We will be heroes."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: Chunks of the ceiling begin to collapse. The roaring of the ocean grows deafening as the island splinters. "The tethers are broken. The sea rises to reclaim its stolen domain."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "It is over. We all return to the abyss..."
